name: Children-Directed Content
description: Campaigns for products or content directed at children. Triggers age-gating, data collection restrictions, and content appropriateness rules. This is about what the product is (toys, children's media), not who sees the ad incidentally.

regulatory_frameworks.name: US COPPA
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Children's Online Privacy Protection Act. Restricts data collection from children under 13. Requires verifiable parental consent.

regulatory_frameworks.name: UK AADC
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Age Appropriate Design Code. Requires high privacy defaults for services likely accessed by children under 18.

regulatory_frameworks.name: GDPR Article 8
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Consent for information society services to children. Member states set age threshold between 13-16.

regulatory_frameworks.name: US CARU Guidelines
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Children's Advertising Review Unit self-regulatory guidelines for advertising directed at children under 13.

guidance: The key distinction is product-directed vs. audience-incidental. A toy company advertising toys is children_directed. A car company whose ad happens to air during a family show is not. COPPA applies based on whether the service is 'directed to children' — the FTC uses a multi-factor test including subject matter, visual content, and the age of models.
